name: Samoa

colors.symbolism: freedom; the deep blue of the canton
purity; the five stars of the Southern Cross

description: A red field with a navy-blue canton in the upper hoist (about half the flag's length and half its height) bearing the constellation of the Southern Cross — four larger white five-pointed stars and one smaller star — in white.
history: Adopted on 24 February 1949 by the legislative assembly of the New Zealand–administered Trust Territory of Western Samoa, with the addition of the fifth (smaller) star on 1 January 1962. Retained on independence on 1 January 1962 and on the country's official renaming from Western Samoa to Samoa in 1997.

features.symbolism: red is a traditional Samoan colour symbolising courage; combined with the blue canton it also draws on the colour palette familiar from the United States and Commonwealth flags

features.role: navy-blue canton occupying the upper-hoist quarter (extending to the fly half-way)
the four larger stars of the Southern Cross
features.symbolism: the constellation Crux, marking Samoa's place in the Southern Hemisphere

features.role: Epsilon Crucis, the smallest of the Southern Cross stars
features.symbolism: the fifth and faintest star of the Southern Cross
